questions.question: Why does React Hook Form use uncontrolled inputs by default?
options: Because it doesn't support controlled inputs
To avoid re-renders on every keystroke, making forms significantly more performant
Because uncontrolled inputs have better accessibility
To avoid TypeScript type errors
questions.answer: To avoid re-renders on every keystroke, making forms significantly more performant
questions.explanation: By using a ref-based approach with uncontrolled inputs, RHF avoids React state updates on every keypress. It only reads form values on submit, drastically reducing renders.

questions.question: What does zodResolver do when used with React Hook Form?
options: Converts Zod schemas to TypeScript interfaces
Connects a Zod validation schema to RHF so validation runs automatically on submit
Resolves import paths for Zod
Creates a Zod schema from a form structure automatically
questions.answer: Connects a Zod validation schema to RHF so validation runs automatically on submit
questions.explanation: zodResolver(@hookform/resolvers/zod) integrates Zod validation with RHF, automatically validating against the schema and populating formState.errors with messages.

questions.question: What is useFieldArray used for in React Hook Form?
options: Creating array-type custom inputs
Managing dynamic lists of form fields (add/remove/reorder)
Validating array fields
Creating multi-select inputs
questions.answer: Managing dynamic lists of form fields (add/remove/reorder)
questions.explanation: useFieldArray provides fields, append(), remove(), move(), insert() and swap() methods for managing dynamic field lists like tag inputs, line items, or team member lists.

questions.question: What is the purpose of setError() in React Hook Form?
options: To throw a JavaScript error
To programmatically add validation errors, typically from server responses
To set the error boundary state
To mark all fields as invalid
questions.answer: To programmatically add validation errors, typically from server responses
questions.explanation: setError('email', { message: 'Email already taken' }) displays a server-side validation error inline on the field, giving users clear feedback without custom error display logic.

questions.question: What does the 'isDirty' flag from React Hook Form formState indicate?
options: The form has validation errors
At least one field value has changed from its default value
The form has been submitted at least once
The form contains unsafe input values
questions.answer: At least one field value has changed from its default value
questions.explanation: isDirty is true when any field's current value differs from its defaultValue. Use it to disable the submit button when nothing has changed, preventing unnecessary API calls.
